subject: English
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: SENSE RELATIONS
topics: Overview: Sense relations in grammar refer to the relationships between words based
on their meanings. These sense relationships are important because they help us
understand how words interact within a language’s semantic system.
activities: synonym is a word or a phrase that has the same or very similar meaning
antonym is a word that has the opposite meaning of another word.
assessment:  Learners identify synonyms and antonyms and write the activity in their books.
 Learners to fill in tables or supply synonyms and antonyms.
 Synonyms and antonyms are two key components of word relations.
 Mastering synonyms and antonyms helps expand vocabulary and enhance ability to
 Synonyms and antonyms are important in understanding language in real life
